Division.title: Division
Division.description: Represents a specific department or area within the Visitor Management System that visitors can check into. This entity defines the properties of each division, including its capacity and display information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Division entity.

englishName.description: The official name of the division in English.

sinhalaName.description: The official name of the division in Sinhala.

backgroundColorHex.description: Hexadecimal color code used for the division's background in the user interface.

textColorHex.description: Hexadecimal color code used for the division's text in the user interface.

maximumCapacity.description: The maximum number of visitors allowed in this division at any given time.

VisitorEntry.title: VisitorEntry
VisitorEntry.description: Records a single instance of a visitor checking into and potentially out of a specific division within the Visitor Management System. It captures details about the visitor and their visit duration.

id.description: Unique identifier for the VisitorEntry entity.

fullName.description: The full name of the visitor as entered during check-in.

identificationType.description: The type of identification provided by the visitor (e.g., NIC, Passport, Driving License).

identificationNumber.description: The unique identification number of the visitor.

address.description: The residential address of the visitor.

divisionId.description: Reference to the Division the visitor is checking into. (Relationship: Division 1:N VisitorEntry)

checkInTime.description: The timestamp indicating when the visitor checked into the division.

status.description: The current status of the visitor, either 'IN' (currently checked in) or 'OUT' (has checked out).

checkOutTime.description: The timestamp indicating when the visitor checked out of the division. This field is present only if the visitor has checked out.

UserProfile.title: User Profile
UserProfile.description: Stores application-specific user data, including roles and custom permissions for authorization.

role.description: The role assigned to the user.

permissions.description: A list of specific actions the user is allowed to perform.

definition.description: Stores static configuration details for each police station division. Each document represents a distinct division with its name, capacity, and display preferences.

params.name: divisionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific police station division.

definition.description: Records individual visitor check-in and check-out events. Includes denormalized 'divisionEnglishName', 'divisionSinhalaName', 'divisionBackgroundColorHex', and 'divisionTextColorHex' for authorization independence and historical data integrity.

params.name: visitorEntryId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific visitor entry record.

definition.description: Stores user profiles and roles for authorization. The document ID must match the Firebase Auth user UID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for a user, matching their Firebase Auth UID.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for the Visitor Management System prioritizes Authorization Independence and Queryability (QAPs) by using two top-level collections: `/divisions` for static configuration and `/visitorEntries` for all visitor records. This approach avoids hierarchical authorization dependencies that require `get()` calls in security rules, making the system more robust and easier to debug.

1.  **Authorization Independence (Denormalization):** To ensure that `VisitorEntry` documents are self-contained and do not require fetching data from `/divisions` for display or authorization purposes, key details of the associated `Division` (such as `englishName`, `sinhalaName`, `backgroundColorHex`, `textColorHex`) will be denormalized directly into each `VisitorEntry` document at the time of check-in. This is crucial for maintaining historical accuracy, as past records will always reflect the division's details as they were at the time of the visit, even if the division's configuration changes later. This also means that security rules evaluating access to a `VisitorEntry` document do not need to perform `get()` operations on the `/divisions` collection.

2.  **Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture):**
    *   The `/divisions` collection stores static configuration data. Its security posture is likely read-only for general staff and writable only by an administrator.
    *   The `/visitorEntries` collection stores all transactional visitor data, regardless of their `status` ('IN' or 'OUT'). All documents within this collection share the same security requirements, typically allowing authenticated staff to read, create, and update entries.

3.  **QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** The single `/visitorEntries` collection, combined with an explicit `status` field (`IN` or 'OUT'), directly supports QAPs. For displaying 'Active Visitors' or 'Visitor History', client applications will issue `list` queries that include a `where('status', '==', 'IN')` or `where('status', '==', 'OUT')` clause, respectively. Firestore security rules can then easily validate that these `where` clauses are present and correct for `list` operations, preventing unauthorized broad data access and ensuring efficient queries without relying on client-side filtering of sensitive data. Because `divisionId` and the denormalized division details are directly within the `visitorEntry` document, no cross-collection `get()` operations are needed to fulfill these list queries, further simplifying rules and improving performance.
